mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series trend upward, or interaction term Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind, triggering margin compression and inventory corrections that force negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power instead of negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth exceeds 2.5%, inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 2.5%, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates labor market tightness and wage inflation, which erodes corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence signal predicts positive returns instead of negative, or the interaction effect Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across multiple rolling evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth falls below 0.5% while real consumption expenditures remain elevated, supply bottlenecks emerge, leading to input cost inflation and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold condition on INDPRO growth does not coincide with negative 20-day returns, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.
